title: Giant anomalous Nernst signal in the antiferromagnet YbMnBi<sub>2</sub>.

paper_analysis.summary: This paper reports a giant anomalous Nernst effect in the canted antiferromagnet YbMnBi2, where weak spin canting breaks time-reversal symmetry and Bi-driven spin–orbit coupling and band dispersion enable large transverse thermoelectric response with very small magnetization. Combining transport, magnetization, ARPES, and first-principles calculations, the authors show an anomalous Nernst conductivity of about 10 A m−1 K−1 and argue that both intrinsic Berry-curvature physics and extrinsic scattering likely contribute.

figures.description: Schematic of a conventional Seebeck thermoelectric device using coupled p- and n-type legs.
Schematic of a Nernst device showing transverse geometry using a single material.
Conceptual band-dispersion comparison between canted YbMnBi2 and a general ferromagnet, emphasizing sharper p–d-derived bands and higher mobility in YbMnBi2.
Crystal structure of YbMnBi2 with Bi1/Bi2 sites and canted Mn spin structure.
Temperature-dependent magnetization in ab-plane and c-axis under field cooling and zero-field cooling, showing TN and spin-canting transition.
Calculated nodal-line band structure in the kx-ky plane without SOC, showing band inversion and symmetry-protected nodal line.
Schematic of how Fermi-level position affects Berry curvature, anomalous Hall conductivity, and anomalous Nernst conductivity.
Magnetic-field dependence of anomalous Nernst thermopower in cb configuration at several temperatures.
Magnetic-field dependence of anomalous Hall resistivity in cb configuration at several temperatures.
Magnetic-field dependence of anomalous Nernst thermopower in bc configuration at several temperatures.
Magnetic-field dependence of anomalous Hall resistivity in bc configuration at several temperatures.
Temperature dependence of absolute anomalous Nernst thermopower at 1 T for cb and bc configurations.
Temperature dependence of absolute anomalous Hall resistivity at 1 T for cb and bc configurations.
First-principles band structure with orbital character decomposition under assumed canting angle.
Calculated Berry-curvature distribution in the Brillouin zone.
ARPES Fermi-surface intensity map at EF and calculated Γ-point band evolution with increasing canting angle.
figures.likely_source: raw measurement / processed for ARPES; derived for calculation inset

figures.description: Calculated absolute anomalous Hall conductivity versus canting angle.
Temperature dependence of absolute anomalous Hall conductivity in cb and bc configurations.
Temperature dependence of absolute anomalous Nernst conductivity in cb and bc configurations, with calculated values at different EF overlaid.
Comparison of |αANE|/M, |SANE|/M, and magnetization M for YbMnBi2 and other large-ANE materials.
Temperature dependence of anomalous Nernst transverse figure of merit zTANE for YbMnBi2 compared with other materials.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: Raw measurements include magnetization data from MPMS3, resistivity and Hall resistivity from PPMS9 four-probe transport, Nernst thermopower/Seebeck coefficient/thermal conductivity from PPMS9 steady-state four-contact measurements, and ARPES spectra from MAX IV Bloch beamline with a Scienta DA30 analyzer. Laue X-ray diffraction and SEM-EDS were used for crystal orientation and composition/homogeneity checks.
paper_analysis.processed_plot_details: The paper plots processed transport quantities such as SANE, ρAHE, σAHE, αANE, and zTANE versus temperature and magnetic field, as well as derived comparisons to literature values. Theoretical plots include DFT band structures, Berry curvature maps, and conductivity versus canting angle or Fermi level.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: The paper states that all data supporting the plots and findings are available from the corresponding author upon request. It also notes that methods, additional references, source data, extended data, supplementary information, and data/code availability statements are available online at the DOI link.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Raw data are described as instrument-collected measurements from PPMS9, MPMS3, and ARPES at MAX IV, plus Laue X-ray diffraction and SEM-EDS characterization. The paper does not specify file formats, repository location, or whether raw files are publicly deposited.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data include extracted transport coefficients and conductivities (SANE, ρAHE, σAHE, αANE), temperature- and field-dependent curves, and derived zTANE values. Theoretical outputs from DFT/Wannier calculations are also plotted as processed/derived datasets.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: The paper implies figure-supporting source data via the Nature Research online materials, but the main text only explicitly says data supporting the plots are available from the corresponding author upon request. Figure-specific source data files are not described in the provided text.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: No analysis scripts, notebooks, or code repository are mentioned in the provided text. Only the use of VASP and Wannier interpolation is described for calculations.
dataset_characterization.replot_reason: Replotting is likely possible for figure data because the paper states that data supporting the plots are available and the figures are standard curves/maps, but the text does not confirm public raw-file access or provide explicit file organization. Thus replot-ready source data is implied rather than fully documented.
dataset_characterization.notes: The paper clearly distinguishes experimental raw measurements from derived transport coefficients and theoretical calculations. However, it does not provide a public repository, explicit raw-data filenames, or scripts. The most likely 'source data' are figure-level numeric datasets for the plotted curves, but this is only indirectly supported by the Nature Materials online-content statement.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: Methods describe direct measurements from PPMS9, MPMS3, ARPES, Laue XRD, and SEM-EDS, indicating raw experimental data were collected.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: The article states that 'all the data supporting the plots within this paper' are available and mentions online source data/extended data, implying figure-level processed source data.
classification_prior.both_evidence: Both raw measurements and figure-supporting processed source data are implied by the Methods and data availability statements.
classification_prior.data_availability_section_relevant: “All the data supporting the plots within this paper and the findings of this study are available from the corresponding author upon request.”; “Any methods, additional references, Nature Research reporting summaries, source data, extended data, supplementary information... are available at https://doi.org/10.1038/s41563-021-01149-2.”
classification_prior.review_notes: Be careful not to equate the plotted transport coefficients with raw measurements: SANE, σAHE, αANE, and zTANE are derived/processed quantities. The paper does not explicitly state that raw data or scripts are publicly deposited; only availability upon request and online supplementary/source data are mentioned.
